course_description:
Students taking this course will have the opportunity to engage in experimental and theoretical skill development in pharmaceutical analysis and quality assurance in pharmaceutics. They will also be exposed to the intricacies of pre-clinical research, how research uses animal models and ethical boundaries. The course will highlight the role of pharmacodynamics, pharmacokinetics and toxicology as well as emphasise the need for collection of safety data in the early stages of drug development. Students will learn about the type of and need for recording results and documentation. The students will meet a career professional and learn about the practical application of the knowledge gained within the course. The theoretical aspects of the course are supported by a large pharmaceutical analysis and pharmaceutical quality assurance individual research project that involves analysis of drug concentrations using industry standard techniques with an emphasis on analytical design, method development and interpretation of findings both in report form and oral defence.

Welcome to the course profile for PHIP7122, a part of the Masters of Pharmaceutical Industry Practice. Please email or see the course co-ordinator for any questions you have regarding the course information.

Additional timetable information

course_aims: This course aims to develop your knowledge and capacity in perspectives of the general theme of preclinical drug discovery, with a specific focus on: preclinical drug and device discovery, animal ethics, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, toxicology, pharmaceutical quality assurance, drug analysis, research learning, and science communication.

learning_outcomes.description: Contextualise the role Preclinical Drug Discovery has in the drug life cycle.
Identify and explain the role of toxicology in light of preclinical and clinical drug evaluation.
Apply the principles of pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics.
Justify the role of animal research and the ensuing ethical considerations and regulatory processes.
Collaborate and apply skills in research learning.
Design and deliver a presentation on scientific research.

assessment_details.title: Poster Presentation of Preclinical Research
assessment_details.due_date: 22/08/2025 - 5/09/2025 Submit the poster by the Friday of the week before your scheduled in-class poster presentation session.
assessment_details.task_description: The poster presentation will focus on preclinical drug discovery topics, including in vitro and in vivo research methods for various diseases, on a topic that will be assigned to each student. Prepare a single PowerPoint slide, formatted as an electronic poster. Give an oral presentation using the poster within the poster presentation session during a scheduled workshop. The schedule for poster presentations will be provided on the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site. Submit your electronic file by the Friday prior to your scheduled time. This task is assessed by two rubrics: one for the oral presentation and one for the poster. 50% of the marks are allocated to each assessment. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for more details, the presentation schedule, and marking rubrics. The presentation will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension independent of AI tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The poster file is to be submitted via Blackboard before the presentation, and the presentation will be in class as scheduled on the Blackboard site.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Pharmaceutical Analysis Research Project Report
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Analyse results from a simulated experiment analysing the active ingredient in a set of tablets. Students will be provided with a data pack in the form of an Excel document that is unique to each individual student. Students will use a graphing program, GraphPad Prism, to determine the contents of the active ingredient within the data set and comment on whether it passes the test. The methods of analysis will be covered in an associated tutorial. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for more details and the marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. assessment_details.title: Pharmacokinetic and Pharmacodynamic Online Quiz
assessment_details.due_date: 29/08/2025 3:00 pm The quiz will be be opened 7 days before the due date.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The quiz will assess the content from the Pharmacokinetics and Pharmacodynamics module within the course. One attempt is permitted for this quiz. The quiz duration is 60 minutes. The format may be a mixture of question types, for example: multiple choice, multiple answer, matching, ordering, fill in the blank/s, jumbled sentence/s, either/or (e.g. true/false), short answer, and short essay style questions. Questions may be provided randomly from a pool, therefore each student may receive a different but equivalent quiz. The quiz will be marked electronically, correct answers will be awarded the marks indicated beside the question, and incorrect answers will be awarded zero. This is an individual assessment that you are required to undertake without any form of communication with other students. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Complete the quiz online via the Link provided at Learn.UQ (Blackboard) any time in the 7 days prior to the submission deadline.

assessment_details.title: Quality Assurance Research Project
assessment_details.task_description: The Quality Assurance research project will be based on the activities conducted during the virtual practical sessions. A dataset will be provided to the students to analyse and write the research report. The report will be assessed against the rubrics from the Blackboard site. Reports are required to address the tasks described in the practical guide and the attached rubric on the Blackboard site. Submit the report via the Turnitin link within Blackboard. See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details and marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: Toxicology This workshop series will cover the concepts of toxicology in the context of pre-clinical drug discovery and its applications in research. The workshop will last 2 hours on the scheduled weeks within the timetable. Learning outcomes: L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2
learning_activities.topic: Preclinical drug discovery This seminar series will cover the practices in preclinical drug research, covering in vitro and in vivo methods for drug development. The seminar duration would be 2 hours per week. Learning outcomes: L01, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 4
learning_activities.topic: English for Academic Communication This workshop session will cover English for academic communication. This workshop will be conducted by UQ College. The duration of this workshop will be 2 hours per week. Learning outcomes: L01, L05, L06
Preclinical Drug Discovery a Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 4
learning_activities.topic: Pharmaceutical analysis This workshop will cover the contents and concepts of pharmaceutical analysis and analytical chemistry in the context of pharmaceutical industry applications. The workshop duration would be 2 hours per timetable session. Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 4
learning_activities.topic: Pharmacokinetics Practicals are held on Wednesday in Week 6 and Weeks 10-13. These sessions will be laboratory based and requires personal protective equipment such as laboratory coat and laboratory safety glasses. The practicals will involve quality assurance assays on pharmaceutical tablets. The practicals are split into two sessions: morning (9am to 12pm) and afternoon (2pm to 5pm). Students will work in groups and submit a group assignment report outlined in the assessment section (Quality Assurance Research Project). Specific dates, time and location are detailed on Blackboard. Learning outcomes: L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 4
learning_activities.topic: Pharmacokinetics This workshop series will cover the concepts of pharmacokinetics and its applications. The workshop will last 2 hours on the scheduled weeks within the timetable. Learning outcomes: L03

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 7 (01 Sep - 14 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Toxicology This seminar series will cover the concepts of toxicology in the context of pre-clinical drug discovery and its applications in research. The seminar will last 2 hours on the scheduled weeks within the timetable. Learning outcomes: L02

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 13 (01 Sep - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Pharmaceutical Analysis and Quality Assurance These are laboratory based sessions and require you to bring your own personal protective equipment, i.e. laboratory coat and laboratory safety glasses. The practicals will involve quality assurance assays on pharmaceutical tablets. The practicals are split into two 3-hour sessions: morning (9am to 12 pm) and afternoon (2pm to 5 pm). Students will be allocated a time within the timetable and is expected to attend the designated time. Students will work in groups to conduct the analysis and prepare a report (Quality Assurance Research Project Report). Learning outcomes: L01, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 9 To Week 13 (22 Sep - 02 Nov)
learning_activities.topic: Research and industry experts Guest lecturers from research and industry will present their experience in their field in the context of pre-clinical drug discovery. The seminar will last 2 hours on the scheduled weeks within the timetable. Learning outcomes: L04, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Laboratory Occupational Health and Safety Learning activities
